title: Battle on Wayland
content:
{{Top|leg}} {{Otheruses|Battle of Wayland}} {{Battle |prev=[[Space Battle at Kuat]]''[[Star Wars: Empire at War]]'' |conc= |next=[[Battle of Fresia]] |image=[[File:Battle_of_Wayland.jpg]] |name=Battle on Wayland |conflict=[[Galactic Civil War/Legends|Galactic Civil War]] ([[Fresian campaign]]) |date=[[1 BBY/Legends|1 BBY]]''[[The Essential Guide to Warfare]]'' places the [[Fresian campaign]] in [[1 BBY/Legends|1 BBY]]. |place=[[Wayland/Legends|Wayland]] |result=[[Alliance to Restore the Republic/Legends|Alliance]] strategic victory and retreat |side1=[[Galactic Empire/Legends|Galactic Empire]] |side2=Alliance to Restore the Republic |side3= |side4= |commanders1= |commanders2=[[Captain/Legends|Captain]] [[Raymus Antilles/Legends|Raymus Antilles]] |commanders3= |commanders4= |unit1= |unit2= |unit3= |unit4= |forces1=*3 [[Bp.2 anti-infantry turret|Anti-Infantry Turrets]] *92 [[Stormtrooper/Legends|Stormtroopers]] *1 [[Scout trooper/Legends|Scout trooper]] in a [[74-Z speeder bike/Legends|74-Z speeder bike]] *3 [[TIE ap-1|TIE Maulers]] *10 [[All Terrain Scout Transport/Legends|AT-ST walkers]] |forces2=*[[R2-D2/Legends|R2-D2]] *[[C-3PO/Legends|C-3PO]] *[[Alliance Special Forces/Legends|Alliance SpecForce]] *15 [[T2-B Repulsor Tank/Legends|T2-B repulsor tanks]] |forces3= |forces4= |casual1=Moderate |casual2=Light |casual3= |casual4= |civilian= }} {{Quote|But, sir, I think my presence there won't be necessary…''"
"''You can't come up with a plan and let someone else take all the credit, Threepio.|[[C-3PO/Legends|C-3PO]] and [[Captain/Legends|Captain]] [[Raymus Antilles/Legends|Raymus Antilles]] discussing the upcoming Alliance operation|}} The '''Battle on Wayland''' was an early battle in the [[Galactic Civil War/Legends|Galactic Civil War]] that was part of a greater [[Alliance to Restore the Republic/Legends|Alliance]] operation to obtain four [[Incom Corporation/Legends|Incom]] [[T-65 X-wing starfighter/Legends|T-65 X-wing starfighter]] prototypes guarded by [[Galactic Empire/Legends|Imperial]] forces stationed on the [[Planet/Legends|planet]] [[Fresia/Legends|Fresia]]. ==Prelude== {{Dialogue|Pilot|Scanners show the Tyranny deployed numerous sentry drones just before it left for Kuat. The Fresian system is locked down!|Antilles|Blast! We'd have the entire Imperial fleet in our laps before we could blink!|C-3PO|Excuse me sir, but I believe Artoo could slice the Imperial station uplink on Wayland and register your ships. Then the Fresian sentries would clear you to the surface.|attr=A Rebel pilot, Captain Antilles and C-3PO|src=}} After a prior [[Space Battle at Kuat|raid]] conducted to lure the [[Imperial I-class Star Destroyer/Legends|''Imperial I''-class Star Destroyer]] ''[[Tyranny]]'' away from Fresia, the Rebel Alliance discovered a network of sentry drones deployed in the [[Fre'ji system]] to warn the Imperial Navy in the eventuality of any attempt to disrupt the Incom Corporation [[Imperialization/Legends|nationalization]] ceremony. With the aid of the [[Protocol droid/Legends|protocol droid]] [[C-3PO/Legends|C-3PO]], the Alliance was able to conceive of a plan to bypass the Imperial sentry network. The [[Astromech droid/Legends|astromech droid]] [[R2-D2/Legends|R2-D2]] would be employed to slice into an uplink station on [[Wayland/Legends|Wayland]] in order to register Alliance ships into the log of attendees of the nationalization ceremony. This would allow the Rebels to reach the surface of Fresia without any interference. ==The Battle== [[File:Interpreting_the_Network.jpg|thumb|left|180px|A Rebel task force defends C-3PO and R2-D2 as they slice into the Imperial network.]] ===Preparation=== The Alliance task force deployed in a forest clearing near the uplink station. R2, C-3PO and a small force of [[T2-B Repulsor Tank/Legends|T2-B tanks]] and [[Alliance Special Forces/Legends|infantry]] prepared to take a path up to the uplink station. They needed to get in and out as quickly as possible. The preparation was critical to the outcome of the battle, as the Rebels could see the uplink station from their landing point. They began to map out a plan, as the Empire was alerted to their presence. ===Engagement=== [[File:Battle_in_the_Woods.jpg|thumb|right|180px|The Rebel Alliance encounters Imperial ground forces in the forest around the uplink station.]] Fighting followed the Rebel advance on the facility. R2-D2 plugged into the uplink station controls and began to steal the codes which would allow the [[Alliance Fleet/Legends|Alliance Fleet]] to bypass the blockade around Fresia. This took some time, however, and the Empire had the opportunity to deploy additional forces. Soon a wave of three [[Stormtrooper/Legends|Stormtrooper]] [[Squad/Legends|squads]] and four [[All Terrain Scout Transport/Legends|AT-ST]] walkers landed to quash the Rebel force. As another wave of three [[Stormtrooper/Legends|Stormtrooper]] squads and four [[All Terrain Scout Transport/Legends|AT-ST]] walkers landed, a final wave of Imperials, consisting of three [[TIE ap-1|TIE Maulers]] and a trio of [[Stormtrooper/Legends|Stormtrooper]] squads landed as well. The Rebels, surrounded by the Empire, began a last-ditch defensive effort to stop them, allowing R2-D2 to steal the codes from the uplink station. The Rebels then disengaged and departed Wayland under fire. ==Aftermath== {{Quote|With the sensors blind to our ships, we should be able to sneak right down to Fresia and grab those X-wings.|Captain Antilles|}} The landing codes stolen from Wayland were accepted as valid by the Imperial forces in orbit around Fresia, allowing the Alliance to land a ground force on Fresia and mount an [[Battle of Fresia|operation]] to liberate the X-wing prototypes. These starfighters would prove be a great boon to the Rebels that would continuously serve well throughout the rest of the Galactic Civil War. ==Behind the scenes== This battle is the second mission of the Rebel campaign in the [[2006]] [[Star Wars video games|video game]] ''[[Star Wars: Empire at War]]''. While players can deploy [[BTL-S3 Y-wing starfighter/Legends|Y-wing]] bombing runs to assist the Rebel soldiers, this is not necessarily required for the success of the mission, and this article does not include them for the sake of factual accuracy. Attempting to finish the last objective of the mission using the game's fast-forward feature can occasionally glitch the mission and render it impossible to finish. A battle being waged at Wayland conflicts with canon established in ''[[The Last Command]]'', as the New Republic did not discover the existence of Wayland until the [[Thrawn campaign]]. [[Holocron continuity database]] keeper [[Leland Chee]] did not address this issue yet.{{SWBoards|url=thread.jspa?threadID=152583&start=960#15881257|thread=Holocron continuity database questions|user=[[Leland Chee|Tasty Taste]]|date=2006-11-03|time=11:05 AM}} ==Appearances== *''[[Star Wars: Empire at War]]'' {{1st}} ==Sources== *[[Star Wars: Empire at War: Prima Official Game Guide|''Star Wars: Empire at War'': Prima Official Game Guide]] {{1stm}} *[[Star Wars: Empire at War: Forces of Corruption Expansion: Prima Official Game Guide|''Star Wars: Empire at War: Forces of Corruption Expansion'': Prima Official Game Guide]] ==Notes and references== {{Reflist}} {{GCW Fresia}} {{Interlang |de=Scharmützel von Wayland |es=Batalla de Wayland (Guerra Civil Galáctica) }} {{DEFAULTSORT:Wayland, Battle on}} [[Category:Battles of the Fresian campaign]] [[Category:Battles of Wayland]]
